ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific term for blended iced coffee frappuccino; 'coffee beverage' (FOODON_03301710) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers coffee-based beverages broadly; no specific frappuccino sub-code exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for blended iced coffee frappuccino beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of a coffee frappuccino is approximately 350 ml (medium cup), equivalent to roughly 350 g, providing about 291 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Coffee Frappuccino (No Whipped Cream) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Coffee Frappuccino (No Whipped Cream), cr_id D08020005, with confidence 0.95. All per_100g values preserved from the HPB canonical record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a coffee frappuccino (no whipped cream) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 53 (low) based on the analogous food 'milk chocolate' and 'sweetened milk-based beverages' from the University of Sydney GI Database, reflecting the dominant sugar and milk carbohydrate matrix. The beverage contains 8.39g carbohydrate per 100g; serving size 350g yields 29.4g available carbohydrate. GL per serving = (53 × 29.4) / 100 = 15.6 (medium GL). Estimation is conservative and clinically defensible given the sugar-milk base. GI 53 is classified as 'low' per University of Sydney GI Database guidelines (GI < 55 is low).

hypertension.key_factor: High added sugar content (7.6g/100g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines, despite low sodium (28mg/100g).
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this beverage due to its 7.6g/100g sugar content, which independently raises blood pressure risk; request an unsweetened or sugar-free syrup version to reduce sugar below the 5g/100g SSB threshold.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 7.6g/100g with only 0.09g/100g dietary fibre classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage; liquid sugar is rapidly absorbed causing acute postprandial glucose spikes.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage as the 7.6g/100g sugar with negligible fibre (0.09g/100g) causes rapid glucose absorption; substitute with unsweetened cold brew or sugar-free versions and monitor postprandial glucose if consumed.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar at 7.6g/100g in a liquid form contributes to hypertriglyceridaemia and endothelial dysfunction; fat content of 4.69g/100g with unknown saturated fat fraction warrants caution.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit this beverage due to its high sugar content (7.6g/100g) which promotes hypertriglyceridaemia; choose unsweetened coffee alternatives and request detailed saturated fat information from the vendor.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar at 7.6g/100g promotes pro-inflammatory mediators, negating the anti-inflammatory chlorogenic acid benefit of the espresso component.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request no sugar syrup to preserve the anti-inflammatory polyphenol benefit of coffee; plain black coffee or Americano is the preferred RA-suitable alternative.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coffee Frappuccino (No Whipped Cream) is a blended iced beverage made from espresso, chocolate syrup, milk, and ice, served without whipped cream. The drink is smooth, creamy, and cold with a sweet coffee-chocolate flavour profile. It is a popular speciality coffee beverage widely available at coffee chains and cafes in Singapore and internationally. The removal of whipped cream reduces the fat content compared to the standard version, but the chocolate syrup contributes a significant sugar load of 7.6g per 100g, classifying it as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB). Per 100g, it provides 83 kcal, 2.35g protein, 4.69g fat, 8.39g carbohydrate, 7.6g sugar, 0.09g dietary fibre, and 28mg sodium. Calcium is notably present at 70mg/100g from the milk component. Clinically, the high sugar content with negligible fibre makes this beverage unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The liquid sugar matrix results in rapid glucose absorption and postprandial glucose spikes. Despite the espresso base providing chlorogenic acids with potential anti-inflammatory properties, the sugar load negates these benefits for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis or inflammatory conditions. A medium serving of 350g provides approximately 291 kcal and 29.4g carbohydrate with a glycemic load of approximately 15.6 (medium). Unsweetened or sugar-free syrup versions are recommended for health-conscious consumers.
common_user_queries: Blended iced coffee drink nutrition Singapore

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D08020005) with a confidence of 0.95. The HPB is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent. GI is estimated from an analogous approved source (University of Sydney GI Database) as no direct measurement exists for this specific beverage.
notes: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific blended coffee frappuccino formulation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (D08020005) or in USDA FoodData Central for an equivalent product as of 2026. GI estimated at 53 (low) using analogous sweetened milk-based beverage data from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for coffee frappuccino exists in the approved GI source. Dietary tags 'low_sodium' and 'low_calorie' from the input record are preserved as contextual tags; 'low_calorie' is relative to full-cream coffee beverages with whipped cream. The food is not classified as a Singaporean dish and therefore multilingual naming requirements (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) do not apply; this is an internationally originating speciality coffee beverage.
